page: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU virtual server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical AMD EPYC Milan cores running at 2.0 GHz, supporting 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provisioned with a 240 GB SSD, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, including Geekbench multi-core compiling and Passmark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 585.69 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 38.5 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For database and web serving workloads, the instance achieves an average of 11.09 million Redis SET operations per second and 11.3 GB/sec static web serving throughput.

The dedicated CPU allocation offers predictable performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core instances, representing a balanced resource density for production workloads. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in complex tasks like LLM prompt processing on medium-sized models, and lacks hardware acceleration for heavy AI workloads. The ccx33 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including software compilation, text processing, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. Operating on the QEMU hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 4 physical cores with multithreading and a 240 GB local SSD, though it lacks integrated GPUs. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution speeds, making it a reliable option for general-purpose applications. It is highly suitable for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, text processing, and static web serving, where predictable compute performance and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio are required.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 8 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a 240 GB local SSD, delivering reliable single-core performance for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated virtual server with 8 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 31.0 GB RAM, and a 240 GB SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources with local SSD storage for predictable production workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors
31.0 GB of system memory and 240 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Dedicated CPU allocation for predictable, non-shared compute power
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
